title: Review the new multi-reply Notion support in /Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto from an operational-risk angle. Focus on production verification risks, sync drift, scheduler behavior, and failure modes. Return findings ordered by severity with file references. Do not edit files.
summary_text: **Findings** - Deployed worker version is current and identifiable: production deployment `cec8aeae-757c-44d5-9921-7eb7fbf482fa` points to version `24229008-4a87-4f8d-af92-fe49394dda56` (created `2026-04-05T13:29:12Z`). - Deployed-code evidence matches that version: Cloudflare script endpoint returned `ETag: 75f5667b...334b7`, which matches `versions view` metadata for version `24229008-...`. - Failover mode is configured as intended: production schedules API returned `{"schedules":[]}` (no cron), so worker is manual failover-only. - Health endpoint is live: `GET https://x-auto-poster.masa-stage1.workers.dev/health` returned `{"status":"ok","time":"22:39"}` on `2026-04-05`. - Failover trigger is protected: unauthenticated `POST /trigger` returned `401 Unauthorized`. - Multi-reply fetch logic is present in deployed code (`Reply URLs` → `replyUrl`, then `split("\n")` reply chain). - Read-only production Notion query (approved rows) found the OECD post (`page_id: 3396b8c0-f0ac-81ba-9461-eb4a8c21e589`) with **2 parsed reply lines**: 1. `https://www.oecd.org/en/publications/scaling-artificial-intelligence-in-health_a436e12d-en.html` 2. `https://newspicks.com/news/16384206/` **Residual risks** - No authenticated `/trigger` execution was performed (intentionally, to avoid accidental posting), so full end-to-end failover posting was not exercised in production. - `/health` is liveness-only; it does not validate Notion/API reachability or version ID in response payload. - OECD verification used a read-only Notion query plus worker-equivalent parsing, not an actual worker-run fetch invocation with production secrets.
